summary.intent: Implement native filesystem tools (Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep) for the moltis project to fix issue #657, replacing shell-based exec with structured AgentTool implementations that provide better model quality, path-level security, and auditability.
summary.outcome: Phase 1 MVP fully implemented and committed across 5 commits on the inexpensive-primrose worktree: 6 native fs tools, typed error taxonomy, absolute-path enforcement with workspace_root builder, ToolRegistry collision warnings, byte-cap on Read output, session-key helper, fs-tools Cargo feature flag, and a registry pipeline contract test suite (75 fs tests total, 761 workspace tests passing).

repo: AgentTool trait defined in crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs:12 — name/description/parameters_schema/execute pattern; ExecTool at crates/tools/src/exec.rs:194 is the canonical template for new tools.
MCP tools are already namespaced as mcp__{server}__{tool} so no collision risk with native tool names; plugin tools don't register as AgentTool instances.

The coding policy profile (crates/tools/src/policy.rs:33) explicitly allows only [exec, browser, memory] — new tool names must be added manually or they are silently blocked for users on that profile.
ToolRegistry::register silently overwrites on duplicate keys with no warning — latent footgun for future builtins or WASM tools.
Gateway Glob/Grep without explicit path default to process cwd (service working dir, not project root) — must require absolute paths or a workspace_root builder param.
tools.policy is name-based glob matching, so deny=[Write,Edit,MultiEdit] yields read-only agents for free once tools are registered by correct names.
crates/config/src/schema.rs + build_schema_map() must be updated for any new config sections per CLAUDE.md rules.
file_io.rs is an internal helper (read_host_file/read_sandbox_file, 20MB cap) used only by send_image/send_document — not an agent-facing tool and not a useful base for fs tools.
Workspace lints deny unused_qualifications — fully-qualified paths like std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound inside match arms will fail CI.

code.finding: AgentTool trait definition — name/description/parameters_schema/execute interface all new tools must implement
ExecTool: 1.7K LOC already wired to ApprovalManager, SandboxRouter, NodeExecProvider, policy.rs — canonical template for new tools
coding profile allows only [exec, browser, memory] — must add Read/Write/Edit/MultiEdit/Glob/Grep or users on this profile silently lose all fs tools
pub mod fs; module declaration added here to expose new fs tools module
register_fs_tools called here next to CalcTool registration; gated behind #[cfg(feature=fs-tools)]

workflow: Reference repos (claw-code=catalog only, openclaw=best for edge-case semantics like adaptive paging/edit recovery/Claude param compat, hermes=best for agent-loop concerns like re-read tracking/path policy) — read all three before designing, copy from none directly.

Match Claude Code parameter names exactly from day one (file_path, old_string, new_string, replace_all, pattern, etc.) to avoid the normalization debt openclaw paid with CLAUDE_PARAM_GROUPS and patchToolSchemaForClaudeCompatibility.
Typed errors should be Ok(Value) with a kind field rather than Err — file-not-found is a normal tool result the LLM branches on, not a crash; only unexpected internal failures stay as Err.

just lint fails on pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build error unrelated to fs tools — run clippy scoped to the target crate instead: cargo +nightly clippy -p moltis-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings.
Phase-by-phase bead structure (epic → phase tasks → discovered-from child beads) with bd create + bd claim pattern keeps implementation organized and surfaces gaps as typed work items.
Atomic writes via tempfile::NamedTempFile::persist are the correct pattern; hermes's stdin-pipe approach is not atomic.
Using grep-regex/grep-searcher/grep-matcher (ripgrep engine crates) instead of subprocess gives native Grep with no shell-out.

friction: bd dolt pull failed at session start (remote 'origin' not found) — beads may be out of sync with shared store; no local fix available.

Metrics API required investigation across multiple files (process.rs, moltis_metrics constants) before the correct counter pattern was clear — had to strip custom metrics and use existing moltis_tool_executions_total pattern.
Workspace lint deny(unused_qualifications) caused compile failures after typed error refactor — required a second pass to remove fully-qualified path prefixes inside match arms.
Clippy flagged collapsible_if and needless_range_loop in grep.rs on first pass — required a second clippy iteration before clean.
just lint is broken workspace-wide due to pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA issue, masking whether lint truly passes; must scope clippy per-crate.
Glob and Grep silently resolved against process cwd when no path provided — discovered only during post-implementation audit, not caught in initial design review.
open_items: moltis-sug — Phase 2: SandboxFsBridge trait + shell/tar round-trip for Edit/Write in sandbox; native Read/Glob/Grep bridge (substantial, blocked on moltis-rtm investigation complete)
moltis-8o1 — Phase 3: adaptive paging (openclaw formula), edit recovery (whitespace-normalized fallback), re-read loop detection (hermes tracker), must-read-before-write invariant
moltis-6co — Phase 4: [tools.fs] config section (allow_paths/deny_paths/workspace_only/max_read_bytes/binary_policy/respect_gitignore/follow_symlinks/must_read_before_write), hook payload docs
moltis-oc4 — P2 investigation: whether Write/Edit/MultiEdit should emit checkpoint events for branch-session to fork cleanly from pre-edit state
moltis-tm8 — P3 prep: wire _session_key through the six tool execute() methods (currently only helper exists in shared.rs, not called by any tool)
Read total output size cap is implemented as a line-count limit but no total-byte budget per tool-response payload — may still blow payload limits on wide files
No benchmark gate: Grep correctness is tested but performance vs rg subprocess on large trees is unknown
WASM-isolated fs tools explicitly deferred as potential future phase 5 — noted on epic description but no bead filed
NotebookEdit tool not in scope for issue #657 — explicitly excluded
Symlink follow behavior on Read is undocumented — silently follows symlinks while Write/Edit/MultiEdit reject them; asymmetry needs a comment or config option
Cross-session read-tracker persistence not addressed — must-read-before-write invariant resets on process restart (documented limitation for phase 3)
Nothing pushed yet — four commits on inexpensive-primrose worktree, merge to main not yet performed
